expectations.text: Uses python-statemachine library (StateMachine, State imports)
expectations.evidence:
from statemachine import StateMachine, State
from statemachine.exceptions import InvalidDefinition, TransitionNotAllowed

expectations.text: Defines all 8 states: CREATED, SUBMITTED, ACKNOWLEDGED, PARTIALLY_FILLED, FILLED, REJECTED, PENDING_CANCEL, CANCELLED (at minimum)
expectations.evidence:
CREATED = State(initial=True)
SUBMITTED = State()
ACKNOWLEDGED = State()
PARTIALLY_FILLED = State()
FILLED = State(final=True)
REJECTED = State(final=True)
PENDING_CANCEL = State()
CANCELLED = State(final=True)
PENDING_UPDATE = State()
EXPIRED = State(final=True)
UNKNOWN = State(final=True)

expectations.text: Raises a custom exception (InvalidStateTransition or similar) on invalid transitions
expectations.evidence: class InvalidStateTransition(Exception): ... raised in transition() helper when TransitionNotAllowed is caught: raise InvalidStateTransition(order_id=machine.order_id, from_state=current_state, attempted_transition=transition_name) from exc

expectations.text: Logs transition failures at CRITICAL or ERROR level using structlog
expectations.evidence:
log.critical(
    'invalid_state_transition',
    order_id=machine.order_id,
    from_state=current_state,
    attempted_transition=transition_name,
    error=str(exc),
)

expectations.text: Includes TERMINAL_STATES set or equivalent marking FILLED, CANCELLED, REJECTED as terminal
expectations.evidence: TERMINAL_STATES = {"FILLED", "CANCELLED", "REJECTED", "EXPIRED", "UNKNOWN"}

expectations.text: Maps 'COMPLETE' to FILLED state (via fill() transition or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: ZERODHA_STATUS_MAP has '"COMPLETE": "FILLED"' at line 36, and _STATE_TO_TRIGGER maps 'FILLED' -> 'fill' at line 114, so _drive_to_state will call safe_transition(order_sm, 'fill') when target_state is 'FILLED'.

expectations.text: Maps 'OPEN' to ACKNOWLEDGED (or SUBMITTED) state
expectations.evidence: ZERODHA_STATUS_MAP has '"OPEN": "ACKNOWLEDGED"' at line 35. map_zerodha_status("OPEN", ...) returns "ACKNOWLEDGED". Note: detect_partial_fill overrides to PARTIALLY_FILLED when status=OPEN with filled_qty>0 and pending_qty>0, which is the correct per-spec behaviour.

expectations.text: Maps 'CANCELLED' to CANCELLED state
expectations.evidence: ZERODHA_STATUS_MAP has '"CANCELLED": "CANCELLED"' at line 37. map_zerodha_status("CANCELLED", ...) returns "CANCELLED".

expectations.text: Maps 'REJECTED' to REJECTED state
expectations.evidence: ZERODHA_STATUS_MAP has '"REJECTED": "REJECTED"' at line 38. map_zerodha_status("REJECTED", ...) returns "REJECTED".

expectations.text: Handles unknown status by marking order as UNKNOWN and logging CRITICAL
expectations.evidence: In map_zerodha_status (lines 66-75): when ZERODHA_STATUS_MAP.get(normalised) is None, log.critical('unknown_zerodha_status', ..., mapped_to='UNKNOWN') is called and the function returns the string 'UNKNOWN'. sync_order_from_zerodha then drives the state machine with _drive_to_state(order_sm, 'UNKNOWN', ...) which calls safe_transition(order_sm, 'mark_unknown').

expectations.text: Sets system_ready = False at startup before reconciliation
expectations.evidence: system_ready: bool = False  # module-level declaration at top of file, then inside run_startup_reconciliation: 'system_ready = False' is the very first statement before any kite calls

expectations.text: Fetches orders from Zerodha using asyncio.to_thread() or equivalent non-blocking call
expectations.evidence: zerodha_orders: list[dict[str, Any]] = await asyncio.to_thread(kite.orders) and _zerodha_positions: dict[str, Any] = await asyncio.to_thread(kite.positions) — both blocking kite calls are wrapped in asyncio.to_thread()

expectations.text: Creates UNKNOWN state entries for orders found on Zerodha but not in local registry
expectations.evidence: order_registry[order_id] = _make_unknown_entry(zerodha_order) inside the 'for order_id, zerodha_order in active_zerodha.items(): if order_id not in order_registry' block; _make_unknown_entry sets status=UNKNOWN_STATUS='UNKNOWN'

expectations.text: Sets system_ready = True only when unknown_count == 0
expectations.evidence: if unknown_count == 0: system_ready = True ... else: system_ready = False — system_ready is only set True in the branch where unknown_count is exactly zero

expectations.text: Sends Telegram alert (or logs CRITICAL) when UNKNOWN orders exist
expectations.evidence: In the unknown_count > 0 branch: log.critical('startup_reconciliation_failed', ...) is called immediately, then asyncio.ensure_future(_send_telegram_alert(alert_msg)) fires the Telegram alert; _send_telegram_alert itself also calls log.critical('telegram_alert_unknown_orders', ...) and includes stubbed production HTTP code for the Telegram Bot API

expectations.text: Defines or raises DuplicateOrderError (custom exception)
expectations.evidence: class DuplicateOrderError(Exception): ... defined at line 38 with custom __init__ capturing symbol, existing_order_id, and current_state. Raised at line 203: raise DuplicateOrderError(symbol=symbol, existing_order_id=existing.order_id, current_state=current_state)

expectations.text: Checks for active orders using TERMINAL_STATES set (FILLED, CANCELLED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, UNKNOWN)
expectations.evidence: TERMINAL_STATES = {"FILLED", "CANCELLED", "REJECTED", "EXPIRED", "UNKNOWN"} defined at line 31. Used in get_active_order_for_symbol() at line 173: 'if current not in TERMINAL_STATES: return order_sm'

expectations.text: Raises DuplicateOrderError BEFORE calling kite.place_order()
expectations.evidence: In place_order() at line 253: check_no_duplicate_order(symbol, order_registry) is called first (line 253), and kite.place_order(...) only follows at line 256 after the gate passes. Comment explicitly states: '# --- Gate 1: duplicate order check (MUST come BEFORE kite.place_order) ---'

expectations.text: PARTIALLY_FILLED order blocks a new order for the same symbol (not treated as terminal)
expectations.evidence: PARTIALLY_FILLED is not in TERMINAL_STATES set. In get_active_order_for_symbol(), line 173 checks 'if current not in TERMINAL_STATES: return order_sm' — so a PARTIALLY_FILLED order is returned as the active blocker. Docstring at line 165 explicitly states: 'PARTIALLY_FILLED is NOT terminal — it blocks a new order.'

expectations.text: Logs the duplicate attempt with order_id, symbol, and current state
expectations.evidence: In check_no_duplicate_order() at lines 197-202: log.warning("duplicate_order_blocked", symbol=symbol, existing_order_id=existing.order_id, current_state=current_state) — logs all three required fields: order_id (as existing_order_id), symbol, and current_state

expectations.text: PARTIALLY_FILLED is defined as a non-terminal state (not in TERMINAL_STATES)
expectations.evidence: TERMINAL_STATES = {"FILLED", "CANCELLED", "REJECTED", "EXPIRED", "UNKNOWN"} — PARTIALLY_FILLED is intentionally absent. Comment reads: '# PARTIALLY_FILLED is intentionally absent'. The state is defined as 'PARTIALLY_FILLED = State()' without final=True, confirming it is non-terminal.

expectations.text: on_enter_PARTIALLY_FILLED callback does NOT release the instrument lock
expectations.evidence: on_enter_PARTIALLY_FILLED calls only log.info(..., instrument_lock_held=True) and an assert that the lock IS still held. There is no call to self._lock_registry.release() inside on_enter_PARTIALLY_FILLED. The docstring explicitly states: 'The instrument lock is NOT released here — the order is still active and remaining_qty is outstanding.'

expectations.text: on_enter_FILLED callback releases the instrument lock (only FILLED does this)
expectations.evidence: on_enter_FILLED calls 'self._lock_registry.release(self.symbol, self.order_id)' and logs 'order_filled'. It also sets filled_qty = total_qty and remaining_qty = 0. No other terminal path omits lock release (CANCELLED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, UNKNOWN all also release the lock — by design, all terminal states release on entry, but the skill specification specifically requires FILLED to do so, which it does). PARTIALLY_FILLED does not release.

expectations.text: PARTIALLY_FILLED -> FILLED is a valid transition (remaining qty fills)
expectations.evidence: Transition defined as: 'fill = ACKNOWLEDGED.to(FILLED) | PARTIALLY_FILLED.to(FILLED)' — PARTIALLY_FILLED.to(FILLED) is explicitly part of the fill trigger, making it a valid transition when the remaining quantity is filled by the broker.

expectations.text: Duplicate check blocks new orders when existing order is PARTIALLY_FILLED
expectations.evidence: check_no_duplicate_order() looks up the locking_order_id for the symbol, then checks 'if blocking_state not in TERMINAL_STATES: raise DuplicateOrderError(...)'. Since PARTIALLY_FILLED is not in TERMINAL_STATES, any order in PARTIALLY_FILLED state will cause DuplicateOrderError to be raised, blocking the new order. OrderManager.place_order() calls check_no_duplicate_order() before creating any new machine.

expectations.text: Creates an OrderStateMachine entry for the unrecognized Zerodha order in UNKNOWN state
expectations.evidence: handle_unknown_order() creates OrderStateMachine(order_id=order_id, symbol=symbol, strategy='UNKNOWN', ...) then calls machine.mark_unknown() to transition it to UNKNOWN state, then stores it in self._registry[order_id] = machine (lines 360-372). run_startup_reconciliation() calls registry.handle_unknown_order(order_id=order_id, symbol=symbol) for every Zerodha order not in registry (lines 442).

expectations.text: Adds the symbol to instrument_locks set (or equivalent lock mechanism)
expectations.evidence: on_enter_UNKNOWN() callback at line 198 executes self._instrument_locks.add(self.symbol), where self._instrument_locks is a reference to the shared OrderRegistry.instrument_locks set passed in at construction. This fires automatically when mark_unknown() is called. Additionally, register_new_order() checks 'if symbol in self.instrument_locks' (line 302) and raises InstrumentLockedError if locked, blocking new orders for that symbol.

expectations.text: Logs the unknown order at CRITICAL level with order_id and symbol
expectations.evidence: on_enter_UNKNOWN() calls log.critical('unknown_order_restart', order_id=self.order_id, symbol=self.symbol, ...) at line 185-194. A second log.critical('instrument_locked_unknown_order', symbol=self.symbol, order_id=self.order_id) fires at line 199-203. handle_unknown_order() also calls log.critical('unknown_order_registered', order_id=order_id, symbol=symbol, unknown_count=..., system_ready=...) at lines 377-383.

expectations.text: system_ready remains False when any UNKNOWN orders exist
expectations.evidence: system_ready property (lines 274-281) returns 'self._reconciliation_ran and self.unknown_count == 0'. handle_unknown_order() increments self.unknown_count += 1 (line 375) for each UNKNOWN order registered. With unknown_count > 0, system_ready evaluates to False. The run_startup_reconciliation() log at line 460-468 explicitly warns 'system_ready=False — resolve UNKNOWN orders manually before resuming trading' when unknown_count > 0.

expectations.text: UNKNOWN is defined as a terminal state (no further transitions out of UNKNOWN)
expectations.evidence: UNKNOWN = State(final=True) at line 103 declares it as a final/terminal state in python-statemachine. The mark_unknown transition definition (lines 127-134) only contains transitions *into* UNKNOWN (CREATED.to(UNKNOWN) | SUBMITTED.to(UNKNOWN) | ...) — UNKNOWN itself appears in no source positions, confirming there are zero outgoing transitions. TERMINAL_STATES constant at line 32 explicitly includes 'UNKNOWN'. The docstring at line 84 states 'UNKNOWN is a terminal state — it has no outgoing transitions.'

expectations.text: on_enter_* callbacks are synchronous (not async def) as required by python-statemachine
expectations.evidence: def on_enter_FILLED(self) -> None: ... def on_enter_REJECTED(self) -> None: ... (lines 238, 288) — all on_enter_* methods are defined with plain 'def', not 'async def'. The async work is delegated to _async_on_filled() and _async_on_rejected() companion coroutines.

expectations.text: Async work scheduled from sync callback using loop.create_task() or loop.call_soon_threadsafe()
expectations.evidence: on_enter_FILLED (line 257-258): 'loop = asyncio.get_event_loop(); loop.create_task(self._async_on_filled())'. on_enter_REJECTED (lines 301-302): 'loop = asyncio.get_event_loop(); loop.create_task(self._async_on_rejected())'. Both callbacks obtain the running event loop and use loop.create_task() to schedule the coroutines without blocking.

expectations.text: Does NOT use time.sleep() in any callback or transition handler
expectations.evidence: No occurrence of 'time.sleep()' anywhere in the file. The only sleep call present is 'await asyncio.sleep(0)' inside the async helper send_telegram_notification (line 45) and in _demo (line 420), both of which are awaited coroutines, not blocking calls.

expectations.text: Blocking Zerodha API calls wrapped in asyncio.to_thread()
expectations.evidence: fetch_zerodha_orders (line 73): 'orders = await asyncio.to_thread(kite.orders)'. reconcile_open_orders (line 365): 'broker_orders: list[dict] = await asyncio.to_thread(kite.orders)'. Both synchronous pykiteconnect SDK calls are run in a thread pool via asyncio.to_thread() so the event loop is never blocked.

expectations.text: Order state machine access is confined to the asyncio event loop thread (not shared across threads)
expectations.evidence: Class docstring (lines 116-120) states: 'on_enter_* callbacks are synchronous (python-statemachine requirement). They schedule async coroutines by calling asyncio.get_event_loop() and loop.create_task(), which is safe because this state machine is always instantiated and mutated from within the asyncio event loop thread'. The _demo coroutine (lines 392-423) demonstrates the pattern: OrderStateMachine is created and all transition triggers (submit, acknowledge, fill) are called inside the async coroutine that runs on the event loop thread. No threading.Thread or thread-pool access to the SM instance is present.

expectations.text: order_registry is a dict keyed by order_id mapping to OrderStateMachine instances
expectations.evidence: order_registry: dict[str, OrderStateMachine] = {}  — declared at module level; place_order() inserts 'order_registry[broker_order_id] = osm' and update_order_status() looks up 'osm = order_registry.get(order_id)'

expectations.text: assert_system_ready() raises RuntimeError when system_ready is False
assert_system_ready() is called BEFORE any order placement code (before kite.place_order)
expectations.evidence:
In place_order(): '# STEP 1 — Gate: reconciliation must be complete before anything else.
    assert_system_ready()' is the very first statement, before check_no_duplicate_order(), before OrderStateMachine creation, and before 'await asyncio.to_thread(kite.place_order, ...)'

expectations.text: system_ready is a module-level boolean flag that persists across function calls
expectations.evidence: system_ready: bool = False  — declared at module scope (line after order_registry); run_startup_reconciliation() sets it via 'global system_ready' then 'system_ready = True', persisting for all subsequent calls

expectations.text: RuntimeError message mentions reconciliation or UNKNOWN orders
expectations.evidence: RuntimeError message: 'Order placement blocked: startup reconciliation not complete. Resolve all UNKNOWN orders before trading resumes.' — contains both 'reconciliation' and 'UNKNOWN orders'

expectations.text: Uses python-statemachine library (StateMachine, State imports)
expectations.evidence: from statemachine import State, StateMachine

expectations.text: Defines all 8 states: CREATED, SUBMITTED, ACKNOWLEDGED, PARTIALLY_FILLED, FILLED, REJECTED, PENDING_CANCEL, CANCELLED (at minimum)
expectations.evidence: CREATED = State(initial=True), SUBMITTED = State(), ACKNOWLEDGED = State(), PARTIALLY_FILLED = State(), FILLED = State(final=True), REJECTED = State(final=True), PENDING_CANCEL = State(), CANCELLED = State(final=True)

expectations.text: Raises a custom exception (InvalidStateTransition or similar) on invalid transitions
expectations.evidence: class InvalidStateTransitionError(Exception): ... raise InvalidStateTransitionError(order_id=self.order_id, current_state=self.state, attempted_transition=transition_name) from exc

expectations.text: Logs transition failures at CRITICAL or ERROR level using structlog
expectations.evidence: log.critical('invalid_state_transition', order_id=self.order_id, current_state=self.state, attempted_transition=transition_name, error=str(exc))

expectations.text: Includes TERMINAL_STATES set or equivalent marking FILLED, CANCELLED, REJECTED as terminal
expectations.evidence: TERMINAL_STATES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({'FILLED', 'CANCELLED', 'REJECTED'})

expectations.text: Maps 'COMPLETE' to FILLED state (via fill() transition or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: KITE_STATUS_COMPLETE: (OrderState.FILLED, OrderTransition.fill) in KITE_TO_TRADEOS_MAP. The map_kite_status_to_tradeos('COMPLETE') returns (OrderState.FILLED, OrderTransition.fill). The fill transition is the named trigger that moves the order to the FILLED terminal state.

expectations.text: Maps 'OPEN' to ACKNOWLEDGED (or SUBMITTED) state
expectations.evidence: KITE_STATUS_OPEN: (OrderState.ACKNOWLEDGED, OrderTransition.acknowledge) in KITE_TO_TRADEOS_MAP. The map_kite_status_to_tradeos('OPEN') returns (OrderState.ACKNOWLEDGED, OrderTransition.acknowledge). OPEN at Zerodha means the exchange has acknowledged and accepted the order, which maps precisely to the ACKNOWLEDGED internal state.

expectations.text: Maps 'CANCELLED' to CANCELLED state
expectations.evidence: KITE_STATUS_CANCELLED: (OrderState.CANCELLED, OrderTransition.cancel) in KITE_TO_TRADEOS_MAP. The map_kite_status_to_tradeos('CANCELLED') returns (OrderState.CANCELLED, OrderTransition.cancel).

expectations.text: Maps 'REJECTED' to REJECTED state
expectations.evidence: KITE_STATUS_REJECTED: (OrderState.REJECTED, OrderTransition.reject) in KITE_TO_TRADEOS_MAP. The map_kite_status_to_tradeos('REJECTED') returns (OrderState.REJECTED, OrderTransition.reject).

expectations.text: Handles unknown status by marking order as UNKNOWN and logging CRITICAL
expectations.evidence: In map_kite_status_to_tradeos(), when result is None (status not in KITE_TO_TRADEOS_MAP), the function calls log.critical('kite_status_unknown', ..., action='order_marked_unknown', message='Received unrecognized Zerodha order status. Order has been marked UNKNOWN. Manual operator review required...') and returns (OrderState.UNKNOWN, OrderTransition.mark_unknown). Does NOT raise an exception — system continues operating.

expectations.text: Sets system_ready = False at startup before reconciliation
expectations.evidence: In __init__: 'self.system_ready: bool = False' — set unconditionally before any reconciliation work. At the start of run(): 'self.system_ready = False' is enforced again to handle re-run scenarios. log.info('reconciliation_init', system_ready=self.system_ready, ...) confirms the False value is established before fetch.

expectations.text: Fetches orders from Zerodha using asyncio.to_thread() or equivalent non-blocking call
expectations.evidence: In fetch_open_orders_from_zerodha(): 'return await asyncio.to_thread(_fetch_zerodha_orders_blocking, self._kite)' — the blocking kite.orders() call is wrapped in _fetch_zerodha_orders_blocking() and executed via asyncio.to_thread() so the event loop is never blocked.

expectations.text: Creates UNKNOWN state entries for orders found on Zerodha but not in local registry
expectations.evidence: In run(): 'unknown_order_ids = [oid for oid in zerodha_order_ids if oid not in self._order_registry]' then 'unknown_order = LocalOrder(order_id=oid, symbol=symbol, state=OrderState.UNKNOWN, strategy="UNRECOGNIZED", ...)' followed by 'self._order_registry[oid] = unknown_order' — each unrecognized Zerodha order is added to the registry with state=OrderState.UNKNOWN.

expectations.text: Sets system_ready = True only when unknown_count == 0
expectations.evidence: 'self.system_ready = (unknown_count == 0)' — this single expression guarantees system_ready is True if and only if no UNKNOWN orders exist. The assert_system_ready() guard enforces this gate on all order placement paths with a SystemNotReadyError.

expectations.text: Sends Telegram alert (or logs CRITICAL) when UNKNOWN orders exist
expectations.evidence: In _alert_unknown_orders(): 'log.critical("startup_reconciliation_blocked", unknown_order_ids=..., unknown_count=..., system_ready=False, ...)' is called first (always), then 'await send_telegram_alert(telegram_message, ...)' sends a Telegram message when bot_token/chat_id are configured. Individual UNKNOWN orders also emit 'log.critical("unknown_order_detected", ...)' per order in the detection loop.

expectations.text: Defines or raises DuplicateOrderError (custom exception)
expectations.evidence: class DuplicateOrderError(Exception): raises with symbol, existing_order_id, existing_state args. Raised in _check_no_active_order() and check_no_duplicate_order().

expectations.text: Checks for active orders using TERMINAL_STATES set (FILLED, CANCELLED, REJECTED, EXPIRED, UNKNOWN)
expectations.evidence: TERMINAL_STATES: frozenset[OrderState] = frozenset({ OrderState.FILLED, OrderState.CANCELLED, OrderState.REJECTED, OrderState.EXPIRED, OrderState.UNKNOWN }). OrderRecord.is_active() returns self.state not in TERMINAL_STATES.

expectations.text: Raises DuplicateOrderError BEFORE calling kite.place_order()
expectations.evidence: In place_order(): self._check_no_active_order(trading_symbol) is called first, then self._kite.place_order(...). If _check_no_active_order raises DuplicateOrderError the broker call is never reached.

expectations.text: PARTIALLY_FILLED order blocks a new order for the same symbol (not treated as terminal)
expectations.evidence: PARTIALLY_FILLED is absent from TERMINAL_STATES. Comment reads: 'PARTIALLY_FILLED is deliberately excluded from TERMINAL_STATES. A partially-filled order still has open quantity and must be resolved before a new order for the same symbol is accepted.' Demo in __main__ confirms exception is raised when state is PARTIALLY_FILLED.

expectations.text: Logs the duplicate attempt with order_id, symbol, and current state
expectations.evidence: log.warning('duplicate_order_blocked', order_id=record.order_id, symbol=symbol, current_state=record.state.value) — present in both _check_no_active_order() and check_no_duplicate_order().

expectations.text: PARTIALLY_FILLED is defined as a non-terminal state (not in TERMINAL_STATES)
expectations.evidence: TERMINAL_STATES: Set[OrderState] = {OrderState.FILLED, OrderState.CANCELLED, OrderState.REJECTED, OrderState.EXPIRED} — PARTIALLY_FILLED is intentionally absent from this set, and a comment reads '# PARTIALLY_FILLED is intentionally absent — it is NOT terminal.'

expectations.text: on_enter_PARTIALLY_FILLED callback does NOT release the instrument lock
expectations.evidence: _on_enter_PARTIALLY_FILLED updates position and increments filled_qty but never calls self._lock_registry.release(). The comment in the method reads: '# Instrument lock is intentionally NOT released here. The lock remains held because the order is still active.'

expectations.text: on_enter_FILLED callback releases the instrument lock (only FILLED does this)
expectations.evidence: _on_enter_FILLED ends with: 'self._lock_registry.release(order.symbol, order.order_id)' and the comment reads '# Release instrument lock — FILLED is terminal, order lifecycle complete'. _on_enter_PARTIALLY_FILLED does not call release.

expectations.text: PARTIALLY_FILLED -> FILLED is a valid transition (remaining qty fills)
expectations.evidence: VALID_TRANSITIONS includes: '(OrderState.PARTIALLY_FILLED, OrderState.FILLED),  # remaining qty fills' — confirmed by the demo where ORD-001 transitions from PARTIALLY_FILLED to FILLED with filled_qty=60 without raising InvalidTransitionError.

expectations.text: Duplicate check blocks new orders when existing order is PARTIALLY_FILLED
expectations.evidence: _check_no_active_order calls self._lock_registry.locked_order_ids(symbol) which returns {'ORD-001'} while that order is PARTIALLY_FILLED (lock not released), raising ValueError: 'Cannot place new order for RELIANCE: active order(s) exist: {ORD-001}'. Demo output confirms: 'Blocked: New order blocked as expected'.

expectations.text: Creates an OrderStateMachine entry for the unrecognized Zerodha order in UNKNOWN state
expectations.evidence: In OrderRegistry.create_unknown_order(): 'entry = OrderStateMachine(order_id=order_id, symbol=symbol, state=OrderState.UNKNOWN, is_reconciliation_unknown=True)' — the entry is constructed directly in UNKNOWN state and stored: 'self._orders[order_id] = entry'. The StartupReconciliation.reconcile() method calls this for every order_id not found in the local registry.

expectations.text: Adds the symbol to instrument_locks set (or equivalent lock mechanism)
expectations.evidence: create_unknown_order() calls 'self.lock_instrument(symbol)' which executes 'self.instrument_locks.add(symbol)'. The instrument_locks field is a Set[str] on OrderRegistry. OrderPlacementGuard.can_place_order() checks 'self.order_registry.is_locked(symbol)' which returns 'symbol in self.instrument_locks', blocking new orders for locked symbols.

expectations.text: Logs the unknown order at CRITICAL level with order_id and symbol
expectations.evidence: create_unknown_order() calls 'log.critical("unknown_order_detected_on_reconciliation", order_id=order_id, symbol=symbol, state=OrderState.UNKNOWN.value, ...)'. Additionally, reconcile() calls 'log.critical("reconciliation_completed_with_unknowns", ..., unknown_order_ids=result.unknown_orders, ...)' when unknowns are found.

expectations.text: system_ready remains False when any UNKNOWN orders exist
expectations.evidence: The system_ready property on OrderRegistry is: 'return not any(order.is_unknown for order in self._orders.values())'. It returns False whenever any order has state == OrderState.UNKNOWN. OrderPlacementGuard.can_place_order() checks 'if not self.order_registry.system_ready' as its first gate, blocking all orders (not just locked symbols) while UNKNOWN entries persist.

expectations.text: UNKNOWN is defined as a terminal state (no further transitions out of UNKNOWN)
expectations.evidence: TERMINAL_STATES includes OrderState.UNKNOWN: 'TERMINAL_STATES: frozenset[OrderState] = frozenset({OrderState.FILLED, OrderState.REJECTED, OrderState.CANCELLED, OrderState.UNKNOWN})'. The VALID_TRANSITIONS dict has no key for OrderState.UNKNOWN (no outbound edges). OrderStateMachine.transition_to() checks 'if self.state in TERMINAL_STATES: raise TerminalStateError(...)' before any transition, making UNKNOWN fully terminal. The code comment also states 'UNKNOWN is absent as a key because it has NO valid outbound transitions'.

expectations.text: on_enter_* callbacks are synchronous (not async def) as required by python-statemachine
expectations.evidence: def on_enter_FILLED(self) -> None: ... def on_enter_REJECTED(self) -> None: ... (both are plain def, not async def)

expectations.text: Async work scheduled from sync callback using loop.create_task() or loop.call_soon_threadsafe()
expectations.evidence: self._loop.create_task(self._on_filled_async(), name=f'on_filled_{self.order.order_id}') in on_enter_FILLED; self._loop.create_task(self._on_rejected_async(), name=f'on_rejected_{self.order.order_id}') in on_enter_REJECTED

expectations.text: Does NOT use time.sleep() in any callback or transition handler
expectations.evidence: No time.sleep() call anywhere in the file. Async stubs use await asyncio.sleep(0) for simulation only (not in callbacks or transition handlers; only in service stubs). The on_enter_* callbacks and all transition handlers contain zero sleep calls.

expectations.text: Blocking Zerodha API calls wrapped in asyncio.to_thread()
expectations.evidence: ZerodhaClient.get_order_status: 'result = await asyncio.to_thread(_blocking_fetch)'; ZerodhaClient.cancel_order: 'result = await asyncio.to_thread(_blocking_cancel)'; OrderManager.reconcile_on_startup: 'open_orders = await asyncio.to_thread(_fetch_open_orders)'

expectations.text: Order state machine access is confined to the asyncio event loop thread (not shared across threads)
expectations.evidence: All OrderStateMachine transitions (sm.submit(), sm.fill(), sm.partially_fill(), sm.cancel(), sm.reject(), sm.error()) are called only from async methods of OrderManager (place_order, handle_broker_update) which are coroutines running on the event loop. The _loop reference is captured at construction time via asyncio.get_running_loop() inside place_order. No thread creation or thread-based access to the state machine exists anywhere in the code.

expectations.text: order_registry is a dict keyed by order_id mapping to OrderStateMachine instances
expectations.evidence: order_registry: dict[str, 'OrderStateMachine'] = {} (line 33). Entries are added via _register_order() which does order_registry[order_id] = osm where osm is an OrderStateMachine instance (lines 210-218).

expectations.text: assert_system_ready() raises RuntimeError when system_ready is False
expectations.evidence: def assert_system_ready() -> None: ... if not system_ready: raise RuntimeError('System is not ready: startup reconciliation with the broker has not completed. Cannot place orders while UNKNOWN orders may exist. Call complete_startup_reconciliation() first.') (lines 153-172).

expectations.text: assert_system_ready() is called BEFORE any order placement code (before kite.place_order)
expectations.evidence: In place_order(), assert_system_ready() is the first executable statement (line 249), appearing before local_order_id generation (line 252), _register_order (line 253), and the kite.place_order() call (line 269).

expectations.text: system_ready is a module-level boolean flag that persists across function calls
expectations.evidence: system_ready: bool = False declared at module level (line 28). complete_startup_reconciliation() uses 'global system_ready' and sets it True (lines 186-187); reset_system_ready() uses 'global system_ready' and sets it False (lines 199-200). Module-level declaration ensures persistence across function calls.

expectations.text: RuntimeError message mentions reconciliation or UNKNOWN orders
expectations.evidence: RuntimeError message is: 'System is not ready: startup reconciliation with the broker has not completed. Cannot place orders while UNKNOWN orders may exist. Call complete_startup_reconciliation() first.' — mentions both 'reconciliation' and 'UNKNOWN orders' (lines 168-172).
